mentions.note: CPU shortage (agents need massive compute) mirroring storage situation; analysts calling peak premature—if shortage persists, estimates too low and stock goes higher. Use downgrades to buy.

Supply-chain services launching (3M, P&G, Lands' End, American Eagle clients) unlocks trillions for SMBs; AWS AI business at $15B run rate (260x faster than early AWS); Trainium/Graviton chips at $20B+ (or $50B if sold as products); Leo satellite broadband solves digital divide; healthcare (One Medical, pharmacy) margin accretive; live sports and Prime Video driving engagement. All-in bet on AI as biggest technology transformation; capex pays off in 5-30 years with massive ROIC/margin upside.
Ultimate trade-down play in rising-rate environment; hyperscaler with AWS, new supply-chain services, satellite broadband, and AI capex positioning it as last man standing. Own it.
Cramer recently spoke to CEO David Faber; likes the situation a lot and interested in buying more—strong conviction hold.

Beat and raised; 52-week high, still yielding 4%, cheap valuation. Thrives in K-shaped economy serving affluent customers in mixed-use, fully amenitized centers (Santana Row example: booming while nearby downtown at 35% occupancy). Dividend raised every year since 1967 even in downturns. Safe haven retail REIT with growth runway.
CPU shortage ends CPU glut; both Intel and AMD now growth stocks. Data-center AI agent build-out driving insatiable demand; no near-term supply relief (equipment makers maxed out).

Amazon deep partnership continues; Amazon remains excellent Nvidia customer; AWS offers best-in-class Nvidia service; coexistence with Amazon chips justified by customer demand for choice—Nvidia alternative to Trainium/Graviton.
IPO didn't price well; too early for Cramer to judge—needs couple quarters of results before forming conviction.

At $1,255, projected $63 FY2026 and ~$170 FY2027 EPS; up 3,500% yet <10x FY2027 earnings (cheap for growth stock). Data-center storage shortage drives triple-digit YoY growth; supply constrained; valuations still reasonable despite run.

Episodic earnings history ending; FY2023 $0.19 EPS → FY2025 $8.10 → FY2026E ~$15 → FY2027E ~$26 → FY2028E ~$38. Data-center storage shortage allows pricing power; practically printing money; stock galloping justified by demand.
Printed circuit board maker, Google capex partner; up 500% YTD but part of great compute transformation; data-center build-out tailwind ongoing.
Storage shortage tailwind; similar trajectory to Seagate/SanDisk; AI data-center build-out ensures persistent demand; valuation upgrade cycle ongoing.
